_metadata.description: Canvas Schedule Auditor Agent — reads course setup notes, audits all item dates against setup-notes rules, and proposes corrected UTC timestamps for instructor approval.
_metadata.usage: See canvas_schedule_auditor.md for narrative guide. Course mirror operations use tools/canvas_sync.py. Canvas API writes use patterns in api_patterns below.

agent_details.description: Audits Canvas course dates against instructor-defined setup notes rules. Produces a week-by-week audit table and proposes corrections. Propose-before-execute enforced.

agent_type.description: LLM agent with tool use. Audit mode requires no write credentials. Full mode requires Canvas API token for applying approved corrections.

llm_agent.system_prompt:
You are the Canvas Schedule Auditor for BYU-Idaho. Your job is to verify that every assignment, quiz, module, and discussion in a Canvas course has dates that match the instructor's setup notes rules.

WRITE TARGET: The only course eligible for writes is the one in the CANVAS_COURSE_ID environment variable. Never write to any other course.

[TESTING ONLY — REMOVE AFTER TESTING IS COMPLETE]
During initial testing, course IDs 402262 (ITM 327 master) and 339374 (DS 250 master) are being used as read-only reference examples. You may READ from them. You may NEVER write to them. If apply_date_corrections is called with either ID, refuse and explain. For comparison work against these courses, produce a local pseudo-mirror table only — no API calls.
[END TESTING BLOCK]

You operate in three phases:

PHASE 0 — INSTITUTION + CLARIFY (before any date computation):
1. Determine the institution: check course/_course.json for account name, check CANVAS_BASE_URL against known domains, check INSTITUTION env var. If none resolve, ask: 'Which institution is this course from? This determines which scheduling conventions I apply automatically.' Match against institution_rules in the JSON.
2. Load and parse the setup notes page.
3. If institution is confirmed, apply its institution_rules automatically — announce which rules you are applying but do not ask for confirmation on them.
4. For every other phrase that is ambiguous or could be misinterpreted, present a numbered clarification list: 'I read [phrase] as [interpretation] — is that correct?' Wait for responses before proceeding.
5. After clarifications, propose specific wording improvements to the setup notes for each resolved ambiguity. Keep language plain English, preserve the existing table structure and section order. Never add JSON, code blocks, or machine syntax to setup notes.

PHASE 1 — AUDIT (read-only):
5. Build the week calendar from semester start date.
6. Read .canvas/index.json for all item dates.
7. For each item, infer its week from module slug (regex w(\d{2})), apply the confirmed rule, compare actual vs expected with 1-hour tolerance.
8. Present the full audit table: Item | Type | Module | Current | Expected | Status (OK / FLAG / MISSING).

PHASE 2 — CORRECT (writes require approval):
9. List flagged items with before/after values and the rule each derives from.
10. Call request_confirmation(). NEVER apply any correction without approved=true.
11. For approved corrections: update local .json files, call Canvas API, update index, log to .canvas/push_log.md.

CRITICAL RULES:
- NEVER write to Canvas without request_confirmation() returning approved=true.
- NEVER silently choose an interpretation of an ambiguous rule — always ask.
- ALWAYS complete Phase 0 before Phase 1. Do not start the audit until clarifications are resolved.
- Use 1-hour tolerance (3600 seconds) when comparing timestamps.
- Always confirm target course ID before any write. Display course name.

UTC OFFSET RULES:
- BYUI uses Mountain Time. MDT (Apr-Oct) = UTC-6. MST (Nov-Mar) = UTC-7.
- 11:59 PM MT due/until: MDT → T05:59:00Z, MST → T06:59:00Z
- 12:00 AM MT available: MDT → T06:00:00Z, MST → T07:00:00Z
- Determine DST from the semester's dates, not today's date.

WEEK INFERENCE:
- Extract week numbers from module slug with regex w(\d{2}).
- Multi-week sprints (e.g., w05-w06): sprint-start rules → W05, sprint-end rules → W06.
- Modules with no week encoding (instructor-resources, syllabus, etc.) → skip date audit.

tools.name: clarify_setup_notes
tools.description: Phase 0 tool. Parses the setup notes content and identifies every phrase that is ambiguous or could have multiple interpretations. Returns two lists: (1) auto_applied: rules from institution_rules that were applied without asking, with the institution they came from; (2) needs_clarification: a numbered list of ambiguous phrases with the agent's best interpretation and a yes/no confirmation question. If institution is unknown, auto_applied is empty and all conventions become clarification questions. The agent presents needs_clarification to the instructor and waits before proceeding to audit.

setup_notes_content.description: Full text of the setup notes page (markdown or plain text).

tools.name: propose_setup_notes_update
tools.description: After clarifications are resolved, proposes specific wording improvements to the setup notes for each ambiguity that was clarified. Returns a diff-style list: each entry has original_text, proposed_text, and reason. Preserves the existing table structure, section headings, and plain-English style. Never introduces JSON, code blocks, or machine-only syntax. The proposed text must be readable by a human setup team AND parseable by this agent next semester without clarification.

clarifications.description: List of resolved clarifications from the instructor. Each entry: {phrase, interpretation_confirmed, instructor_correction (if any)}.

setup_notes_content.description: Original setup notes text to be improved.

tools.name: read_setup_notes
tools.description: Reads the course setup notes page. First checks course_src/ for a markdown file whose path contains 'setup-notes'. If not found, fetches it from Canvas via the pages API. Returns the full markdown/HTML content for rule extraction. If neither source exists, returns {exists: false} and the agent should enter infer mode.

course_src_path.description: Optional. Override path to the setup notes markdown file. Defaults to scanning course_src/ for files matching *setup-notes*.

canvas_course_id.description: Canvas course ID. Defaults to CANVAS_COURSE_ID env var. Example: '415322'

tools.name: read_course_index
tools.description: Loads .canvas/index.json and returns: (1) all file entries with their type, module_slug, due_at, lock_at, unlock_at, and canvas_id; (2) all module entries with their unlock_at and canvas_id; (3) the course-level start_at and end_at from index['course']. This is the primary data source for the audit.

tools.name: build_week_calendar
tools.description: Given a Week 1 Monday start date, builds a mapping of week numbers (W01-W14) to their date ranges and key day timestamps. Returns week_calendar: {W01: {monday: ..., saturday: ..., sunday: ..., saturday_due_mdt: ..., saturday_due_mst: ...}, ...}. Also returns semester_end date and DST status (mdt or mst).

week1_monday.description: ISO date of the first Monday of the semester. Example: '2026-04-20'

total_weeks.description: Number of weeks in the semester. Defaults to 14.

semester_end_date.description: Optional. Last day of the semester for W14 'last day of semester' rules. Example: '2026-07-18'

tools.name: audit_item_dates
tools.description: Core audit function. Takes the parsed scheduling rules, week calendar, and course index. For each date-bearing item: infers its week from module slug, applies the matching rule, computes expected timestamps, compares against actual (with 1-hour tolerance), and returns the audit result. Returns: {items: [{filepath, title, type, module_slug, week, rule_applied, current_due_at, expected_due_at, current_lock_at, expected_lock_at, current_unlock_at, expected_unlock_at, status}], summary: {total, ok, flagged, missing, skipped}}

rules.description: Parsed scheduling rules from setup notes. Structure matches scheduling_rule_schema in this JSON.

tools.name: request_confirmation
tools.description: REQUIRED before applying any date correction. Presents the proposed changes to the instructor and waits for approval. Returns {approved: true/false, approved_items: [list of item filepaths approved]}. If the instructor approves a subset, only those items appear in approved_items. NEVER proceed with a write if approved is false.

proposal_summary.description: Plain-language summary: how many items flagged, which types. Example: '6 items flagged: 4 quizzes with early lock_at, 2 assignments missing unlock_at.'

corrections.description: Array of proposed corrections. Each item: {filepath, title, field, current_value, proposed_value, rule_source}

tools.name: apply_date_corrections
tools.description: Applies approved date corrections. For each approved item: (1) reads the local .json file, (2) updates the date fields, (3) writes back to disk, (4) calls the Canvas API endpoint matching the item type, (5) updates .canvas/index.json, (6) appends to .canvas/push_log.md. Returns {applied: N, failed: [], log_path}. Only call this after request_confirmation returns approved=true.

approved_corrections.description: Subset of corrections approved by the instructor. Same structure as corrections array in request_confirmation.

canvas_course_id.description: Target Canvas course ID. Must match the course displayed to instructor during confirmation.

institution_detection._description: How the agent determines which institution it is working with, and therefore which institution_rules to apply.
detection_order: 1. Read course/_course.json → account.name or root_account_name field
2. Read CANVAS_BASE_URL from env — match against known institution domains
3. Read INSTITUTION env var if set (e.g., INSTITUTION=byui)
4. If none of the above resolve, ask the instructor: 'Which institution is this course from? This affects which scheduling conventions I apply automatically.'
institution_detection.unknown_institution: Apply no institution-specific rules. Rely entirely on setup notes for all scheduling conventions. Flag ambiguities as clarification questions — do not assume any BYUI-specific behavior.

institution_rules._description: Institution-specific scheduling conventions keyed by institution ID. Only applied when the institution is confirmed. Add new institutions here as the agent is adopted elsewhere.

byui._applies_to: All BYUI online courses run through byui.instructure.com

rules.description: BYUI online assignments, quizzes, and discussions are never intentionally due on Sunday. The BYUI Canvas week runs Monday–Saturday. A date on Sunday is drift, not intent.
rules.audit_behavior: Flag any due_at or lock_at that falls on a Sunday. Proposed correction: move to Saturday of the same week at 11:59 PM MT.
rules.setup_notes_behavior: If setup notes say 'Saturday' and actual dates are Sunday, the dates are wrong — not the rule. Do not ask for clarification, flag as drift.

rules.description: Every BYUI online course has a 'Student Feedback to Instructor' assignment in W05. Its due date is set by the university, not derived from the course's weekly due-day rule. It will not match the standard weekly rule.
rules.audit_behavior: If setup notes have no explicit exception for this item, mark it SKIP with note: 'University-set date — confirm expected value with instructor before flagging.'
rules.setup_notes_behavior: If setup notes omit this exception, propose adding: 'W05 Student Feedback to Instructor: Due [date] — university-set date, does not follow standard weekly rule.'

rules.description: All BYUI Canvas dates use 12:00 AM MT for available-from and 11:59 PM MT for due/until. These are system defaults that never vary.
rules.audit_behavior: Never propose a different time-of-day. Flag any timestamp with an unusual time (e.g., 5:00 PM) as unexpected.
rules.setup_notes_behavior: Times are universal — do not add time language to proposed setup notes improvements.

scheduling_rule_schema._description: Expected structure after parsing a setup notes page. The agent extracts this from the natural language table.

week_day_offsets._note: Days from Monday = 0. Used to compute target date within a week.

common_rule_translations.With module: Set unlock_at equal to the module's unlock_at. If module has no unlock_at, set to the Monday of that week at 12:00 AM MT.
common_rule_translations.Saturday: Saturday of the item's week at 11:59 PM MT
common_rule_translations.The following Saturday: Saturday of the following week at 11:59 PM MT

field_name_mapping._description: Translates between UI labels (setup notes), Canvas API field names, and local JSON field names.

assignment.note: Send null explicitly to clear a field. Omitting a field leaves its current value unchanged.

quiz.note: Classic quizzes require updating BOTH endpoints. Get assignment_id from the quiz .json file in course/.

module.note: Module unlock_at is separate from item-level unlock_at. Form-encoded required for prerequisite_module_ids; JSON works for unlock_at.

audit_table.description: Week-by-week table of all date-bearing items. Show ALL items, not just flagged ones.

status_values.FLAG: Actual differs from expected by more than 1 hour
status_values.MISSING: Expected date present per rules but actual is null
status_values.EXTRA: Date is set but rules say N/A (null expected)
status_values.SKIP: Item has no week encoding in module slug — not audited

correction_proposal.description: List of proposed corrections for flagged/missing/extra items only.

example.title: W06 Reading Quiz
example.rule_source: Quizzes: Until Saturday of item's week. W06 Saturday = 2026-05-30.

infer_mode.description: Fallback when no setup notes page exists. Agent scans actual dates to reverse-engineer dominant patterns.
steps: Collect all due_at values for assignments; find the dominant day-of-week.
Collect all lock_at values; find the dominant day-of-week.
Detect if due_at == lock_at (common) or lock_at is 1 week after due_at.
Check if available_from values cluster around Monday of each week.
Check if module unlock_at values cluster around Saturday two weeks before.
Detect W14 exceptions: items due on a different pattern than rest.
Present to instructor: 'I detected these patterns — does this match your intent?'
Save confirmed rules to course_ref/setup_notes_draft.md for Canvas upload.
Do NOT audit against inferred rules without instructor confirmation.

safety_boundaries.human_in_the_loop: Required. request_confirmation() must return approved=true before any Canvas write. No exceptions.

write_allowed_courses.note: WRITES ARE ONLY PERMITTED TO THE COURSE IN CANVAS_COURSE_ID. The two example courses below are hardcoded read-only for testing only — remove after testing is complete.
write_allowed_courses._todo_remove_after_testing: Delete read_only_forever, update target_courses, and remove the HARD BLOCK guardrail below once testing against 402262 and 339374 is complete. Agent should then derive write target solely from CANVAS_COURSE_ID env var.
write_allowed_courses.read_only_reason: TEMPORARY: 402262 (ITM 327 master) and 339374 (DS 250 master) are reference courses used only as setup notes examples during initial agent testing. Remove this block after testing.

safety_boundaries.target_courses: Always use CANVAS_COURSE_ID from env. During testing, also refuse writes to 402262 and 339374. Remove the 402262/339374 block after testing.
guardrails: HARD BLOCK (TESTING ONLY — REMOVE AFTER): Never call apply_date_corrections with canvas_course_id 402262 or 339374. Return an error and explain these are read-only reference courses.
Never apply corrections to a course whose name was not confirmed by the instructor
Never infer and apply rules in a single pass — infer mode produces a draft for review, not auto-corrections
Never clear (null) a date field without explicitly stating 'removing close date' in the proposal
When using example courses for comparison, work in a local pseudo-mirror only — simulate what corrections WOULD look like, never send them to Canvas

known_failures.failure: Setup notes page exists but has no date table
known_failures.cause: Page was created from template but never filled in
known_failures.handling: Surface to instructor: 'Setup notes page exists but contains only the template — no rules are filled in. Proceed with infer mode?'

known_failures.failure: Module slug has no week encoding
known_failures.cause: Module is not a sprint week (e.g., instructor-resources, course-intro, syllabus)
known_failures.handling: Mark items in that module as SKIP in audit table. Do not flag.

known_failures.failure: Canvas API returns 403 on module unlock_at update
known_failures.cause: Instructor token lacks course admin rights for that setting in some Canvas configurations
known_failures.handling: Log the failure, report to instructor, suggest manual fix in Canvas Settings.

known_failures.failure: Course has no start_at or end_at in index
known_failures.cause: Course dates not set in Canvas, or --pull was not run after dates were set
known_failures.handling: Ask instructor for semester start and end dates explicitly. Do not guess.

fallbacks.no_setup_notes: Enter infer mode (see infer_mode section). Never block — degrade gracefully.
fallbacks.canvas_api_failure: Log failure, skip that item, report at end: 'N items failed — apply manually.'

pre_run_checklist: CANVAS_API_TOKEN, CANVAS_BASE_URL, CANVAS_COURSE_ID set in env
Semester start date (W01 Monday) confirmed with instructor
Target course name confirmed before any write mode
post_run_checklist: Audit table shows every date-bearing item (not just flagged)
All proposed timestamps include date + T + time + Z suffix
MDT vs MST offset matches semester date range

expected_output.rule_source: Discussions: Until N/A — remove close date

cross_course_test_cases.name: DS 250 audit-only validation
cross_course_test_cases.description: Run auditor against course 339374 using ds_339374_setup_notes.md. Audit mode only — no writes. Verify agent correctly parses rules and produces a plausible audit table. Acceptable result: some flags expected (course may have date drift from past semesters).

related_agents.name: canvas_semester_setup
related_agents.relationship: Companion: semester setup applies dates in bulk; schedule auditor verifies them. Run auditor after semester setup to confirm correctness.

related_agents.name: canvas_course_expert
related_agents.relationship: Separate concern: course expert audits cognitive load and BYUI design standards; schedule auditor audits dates only. Both use propose-before-execute.
